hero.title: 3 Days Exploring
hero.titleAccent: The Lost City of Angkor & Khmer Empire.
hero.intro: The essential Cambodia experience in three focused days. Walk the stone galleries of Angkor Wat at sunrise, stand before the Bayon's 200 stone faces in Angkor Thom, and find the tree-root-split walls of Ta Prohm exactly as they appeared in Tomb Raider. The full Angkor archaeological circuit — the world's largest collection of religious monuments — in one extraordinary day.
hero.heroImageAlt: Angkor Wat temple reflection sunrise Siem Reap Cambodia

includes: Meals as per itinerary (B = Breakfast, L = Lunch)
All tours and sightseeing as per itinerary
excludes: Travel insurance (ETIQA Area 2 Gold from RM41)

overview.heading: 3 Days Cambodia: The Full Picture
body: This three-day itinerary is built around one goal: seeing Angkor properly. Most visitors to Cambodia come specifically for the Angkor Archaeological Park, and this package gives you everything the park contains — the main Angkor Wat temple, the walled royal city of Angkor Thom, the face-tower Bayon, the jungle-reclaimed Ta Prohm, and the pyramid sunset temple of Pre Rup — without rushing any of it.

Day one is reserved for arrival and orientation in Siem Reap: the city's Buddhist temples, the Royal Gardens, and a gentle introduction to Khmer culture before the temples begin. Day two is the full Angkor circuit — a long, deeply rewarding day that most travellers call the single best day of any trip to Southeast Asia. Day three begins with breakfast and a free morning before the airport transfer.

For a first visit to Cambodia, this is the correct package. Three days is enough to see Angkor thoroughly, spend an evening on Pub Street, and leave with a clear understanding of what the Khmer Empire built here and why it matters.

highlights.heading: Why This 3-Day Cambodia Package Stands Apart

items.title: Angkor Wat — The World's Largest Religious Monument
items.body: Built by Khmer King Suryavarman II in the early 12th century, Angkor Wat covers more than 400 acres. The 350-metre causeway approach over the moat, the bas-relief galleries depicting 800 metres of carved Hindu mythology, and the five lotus towers at the centre rising 65 metres — this is one of the greatest architectural achievements in human history. A full morning here barely scratches the surface.
items.imageAlt: Angkor Wat temple towers morning light reflection Cambodia

items.title: Angkor Thom & The Bayon — The Great Royal City
items.body: Angkor Thom is not a temple but a walled royal city covering nine square kilometres. Enter through the South Gate's causeway flanked by 54 stone gods and 54 demons. At the city's centre, the Bayon temple rises with 54 towers each carved with four enormous smiling faces — over 200 faces gazing in all directions. The Terrace of Elephants and the Terrace of the Leper King complete this extraordinary complex.
items.imageAlt: Bayon temple stone faces Angkor Thom Cambodia Khmer

items.title: Ta Prohm — The Tomb Raider Temple
items.body: Built in 1186 as a Buddhist monastery, Ta Prohm was deliberately left partly unrestored. Massive silk-cotton and strangler fig tree roots have been allowed to remain exactly as they were found — splitting stone walls, lifting flagstones, and creating the famous scenes that appeared in Lara Croft: Tomb Raider. Unlike the carefully restored temples nearby, Ta Prohm feels like a genuine rediscovery.
items.imageAlt: Ta Prohm temple tree roots Tomb Raider filming location Cambodia

items.title: Pre Rup — The Classic Angkor Sunset
items.body: A Hindu pyramid of brick, laterite and sandstone built in 961 AD. Pre Rup rises on three tiers to five lotus towers at the summit. Every evening, visitors climb to the upper level to watch the sun drop behind the jungle to the west, with the entire Angkor plain stretching out in all directions. It is one of the most consistently rewarding hours of any Cambodia trip.
items.imageAlt: Pre Rup temple sunset Angkor Cambodia pyramid

days.title: Arrival Siem Reap — City Discovery Tour
days.description: Arrive at Siem Reap International Airport and transfer to hotel. Afternoon city orientation tour: Wat Preah Prom Rath (considered the most beautiful temple in Siem Reap city), the twin Preah Ang Chek and Preah Ang Chorm Shrines (the Sisters Temple), Royal Gardens, Wat Thmei Killing Field Memorial, and the War Memorial Monument. Optional afternoon activities available at own cost: countryside cooking class, sunset ATV tour, countryside bike tour, or Tonle Sap floating village tour. Evening at leisure on Pub Street and the Angkor Night Market — the first introduction to Cambodia's famous street food, silk scarves and silver jewellery.
suggestedActivities: Siem Reap airport arrival & hotel transfer
Wat Preah Prom Rath city temple
Preah Ang Chek & Preah Ang Chorm Shrine
Pub Street & Angkor Night Market evening

days.title: Full Day Angkor Archaeological Circuit
days.description: An early start for the full Angkor temple circuit — the greatest single day of sightseeing in Southeast Asia. Begin with Angkor Wat itself, the world's largest religious monument: the 350-metre causeway approach, the outer gallery bas-reliefs depicting Hindu mythology, and the five central towers at sunrise. Cross to Angkor Thom via the South Gate — the causeway of gods and demons guarding a nine-square-kilometre royal city. Visit the Bayon temple with its 200 carved stone faces on 54 towers, the Baphuon temple pyramid, the Terrace of Elephants (350 metres of royal viewing platform), and the Terrace of the Leper King. Afternoon: Ta Prohm (the Tomb Raider temple) with its tree roots splitting ancient stone, the double-walled Banteay Kdei monastery, and the Srah Srang royal bathing reservoir. End the day at Pre Rup for one of the most celebrated sunsets in Asia. Evening free to explore Pub Street and Night Market.
suggestedActivities: Terrace of Elephants & Terrace of the Leper King

days.title: Morning at Leisure — Depart Siem Reap
days.description: Morning at leisure for personal activities or last-minute souvenir shopping. Siem Reap's Old Market (Psar Chas) is the best source for silk scarves, silver jewellery, handmade crafts, and Cambodian spices. Transfer to Siem Reap International Airport for onward journey.

Dhesu has been arranging Cambodia packages for Malaysian travellers since 1988. We know all of this.

sections.heading: 3 Day Cambodia Tour Package from Malaysia
sections.body:
A 3-day Cambodia tour from Malaysia focused entirely on Angkor is the single most efficient way to experience the world's greatest archaeological complex. Three days gives you enough time for a proper city orientation on arrival, a full unrushed day at the Angkor temples, and a relaxed morning before departure. This is the most popular duration for Malaysian travellers making their first visit to Cambodia.

The Angkor Archaeological Park covers 400 square kilometres and contains more than 1,000 temples. The main circuit — Angkor Wat, Angkor Thom, the Bayon, Ta Prohm, Banteay Kdei, Srah Srang, and Pre Rup — takes a full day and represents the highlights that define why Cambodia draws visitors from around the world.

sections.heading: Cambodia Visa Requirements for Malaysians
sections.body:
Malaysian passport holders require a visa to enter Cambodia. The Cambodia e-Visa can be applied for online before departure (approximately USD 36) or obtained on arrival at Siem Reap International Airport (approximately USD 30, cash only). The process is straightforward and takes around 15-30 minutes on arrival.

Dhesu provides detailed pre-departure information on visa options, Angkor pass purchases, and what to expect at Siem Reap airport as part of every Cambodia package booking.

sections.heading: Is 3 Days Enough for Cambodia?
sections.body:
Three days is the minimum recommended for a Cambodia visit and is entirely sufficient for a thorough, meaningful Angkor experience. The park itself is not rushed in a single day if your focus is the main circuit — Angkor Wat, Angkor Thom, Ta Prohm, and Pre Rup. You will not cover every temple in the complex, but you will see everything that matters most.

If you want to add Tonle Sap Lake or the sacred Phnom Kulen mountain, we recommend upgrading to our 4-day packages. If you want to include Phnom Penh and the Mekong River sunset cruise, our 4-day and 5-day combined packages cover both cities.

items.question: Do Malaysians need a visa for Cambodia?
items.answer: Yes. Malaysian passport holders require a visa to enter Cambodia. You can apply for a Cambodia e-Visa online before departure (approximately USD 36, processed in 3 business days) or obtain a visa on arrival at Siem Reap International Airport (approximately USD 30, cash only, takes 15-30 minutes). Dhesu provides full pre-departure guidance on the visa process for every Cambodia booking.

items.question: How much is the Angkor Archaeological Park entrance fee?
items.answer: The Angkor pass is separate from the tour package cost. A 1-day pass costs USD 37, a 3-day pass (valid over 10 days) costs USD 62, and a 7-day pass (valid over 30 days) costs USD 72. For a 3-day itinerary, you only visit Angkor on Day 2, so a 1-day pass is included in the tour. If you wish to add a dawn sunrise visit on Day 1, a 2-day pass can be arranged.

items.question: What is the best time to visit Angkor Wat?
items.answer: For the classic Angkor Wat reflection photograph, arrive before sunrise (around 5:30am) when the towers reflect in the moat and the light is extraordinary. For comfortable sightseeing, the cooler months of November to February are ideal. The wet season (June to October) has fewer tourists, lush green surroundings, and the temples are quieter — but afternoon rain showers are common. March to May is the hottest period.

items.question: Is this a private or group tour?
items.answer: This is a 100% private tour. You have your own licensed English-speaking guide and private vehicle for all 3 days. The itinerary is yours to follow at your own pace. Dhesu does not run group departures — every Cambodia trip is privately arranged.

items.question: Can I arrange halal food in Siem Reap?
items.answer: Yes. Halal meals can be arranged throughout your stay at no extra cost. Siem Reap has several halal restaurants, particularly in the area around the Old Market. Masjid AN NEAKMAH in Siem Reap is a short distance from the hotel area and can be included in your itinerary on request.

items.question: What should I wear at the temples?
items.answer: Temple dress code applies throughout the Angkor Archaeological Park. Both men and women must cover their shoulders and knees — sleeveless tops, shorts above the knee, and short skirts are not permitted at many temples, particularly Angkor Wat. Light, loose-fitting clothing in breathable fabric is recommended. Comfortable walking shoes are essential. A hat and sunscreen are highly advisable.
